hypothesis: A Rust TUI/GUI app reading Demerzel's state/ directory can provide real-time governance visibility — belief health, evolution trends, compliance status — more effectively than manual file inspection

plan.description: Build a Rust app (likely in ix as a new crate ix-dashboard or standalone) that reads Demerzel state files and renders: belief dashboard (T/F/U/C with confidence), evolution timeline, cross-repo compliance matrix, PDCA tracker, gap alerts
plan.success_criteria: App can read state/beliefs/*.json and render a usable display. MVP: TUI with egui stretch goal.
risks: Scope creep — start with TUI, don't jump to full GUI
ix dependency bloat — keep dashboard crate lean
State format changes — use schema validation
